caption: Funnel-routing crowding near the hill (#979): the default battle's two enemy Cavalry regiments (uid8 west of the hill, uid9 spawning inside the hill's own x-range) both round the hill's shorter, west side on their way to the player line. Before this fix, PathField's funnel corner was a pure function of the blocking rect's geometry and each unit's own clearance -- two same-type regiments computed the literal identical waypoint, and since same-team crowding is resolved only by soldier-body collision (not the regiment-level separation, which now only pushes ENEMY pairs apart), the two blocks jammed into each other for well over a thousand physics ticks before crowd pressure eventually broke the tie (confirmed absent on the commit before the funnel fix, via a direct state-dump comparison against this exact scenario: pre-fix, uid8 was still stuck around y~770 at tick 900 -- barely past its own spawn; with the fix it's at y~310, essentially at the player line). This clip shows the fixed behavior: both regiments keep visibly advancing the whole time, one reaching melee by tick 900 while the other is still closing. The improvement is not total -- uid9 (the unit funneled into the tighter, more contested lane) still shows a real, if brief, blob of compressed/overlapping soldiers around tick 480 as the two blocks squeeze past each other (algorithmic defect scan: uid9 fails the overlap and shape_residual checks in this clip's own sampled window; uid8 passes cleanly). Trading a near-permanent stall for a shorter, visible crowd surge is a clear net improvement, not a full resolution of the underlying same-team congestion.
